summary: Frontend-only verification of Track Customer Return wording/color fix in ReturnsTab.jsx (Credit vs Debit). Logged in as CEO admin (surya.yadavalli@nylaairwater.earth), navigated to distributor Brian → Returns tab. Verified the existing CREDIT-type return (RET-2026-0002) renders emerald-colored 'Total Credit', 'Credit/Unit', 'Total Credit', 'Credit Note Issued' box and 'Create Credit Note' / Credit-Issued status badge with no debit wording leakage. Created a new MISSING/DEBIT-type return via the Create dialog and confirmed: (a) create form switches header to 'Est. Debit' / footer to 'Total Estimated Debit' in amber when 'missing' is selected; (b) the resulting detail dialog (RET-2026-0004, Draft) shows 'Total Debit', amber accent for amounts, items table headers 'Debit/Unit' & 'Total Debit', status badge 'Draft' and footer action 'Create Debit Note'. Also visually confirmed list grid amber amount for the missing row, summary card 'Total Value' label and 'Amount' column header. 16/18 automated assertions passed; the 2 misses are artifacts of a duplicate data-testid (see code review) — not behavior failures (confirmed via screenshots).
action_items: (Low) ReturnsTab.jsx line 468 uses data-testid="returns-tab" on the inner Card while DistributorDetail.js line 2614 already uses the same testid on the TabsTrigger. Rename the Card testid (e.g. 'returns-tab-content' or 'returns-card') to avoid duplicate selectors during automation.
critical_code_review_comments: Conditional wording in ReturnsTab.jsx uses derived const isMissingReturn = selectedReturn?.return_type === 'missing' at the top of the component scope. This works because selectedReturn is state. Behavior verified end-to-end for both 'returned' and 'missing'.

STATUS_BADGES label transformation uses .replace(/Credit/g, 'Debit') for missing-type status badge — correctly produces 'Debit Note Created' for approved missing returns. For 'credit_issued' status of a missing return the label would become 'Debit Issued' which is acceptable.
Duplicate data-testid 'returns-tab' on TabsTrigger and Card root is a minor testability issue (see action_items).

success_rate.frontend: 100% (16/18 automated + visual confirmation of remaining 2 via screenshots)

test_credentials: CEO admin: surya.yadavalli@nylaairwater.earth / test123 (tenant nyla-air-water). Created data: customer return RET-2026-0004 (Empire Restaurant, missing type, qty 2, ₹90) at distributor Brian — left in Draft, not approved.
seed_data_creation: Created one Draft customer return RET-2026-0004 (return_type=missing, total ₹90, account Empire Restaurant) under distributor Brian to enable debit wording validation. Safe to leave for future testing or delete via the row delete action.
context_for_next_testing_agent: RET-2026-0004 (Draft, missing/debit) and RET-2026-0002/0001 (credit_issued) exist under Brian distributor. Use these to re-verify the wording/color split. The duplicate data-testid 'returns-tab' (Card root inside ReturnsTab.jsx vs TabsTrigger in DistributorDetail.js) can trip up text-content selectors — query inside [role='dialog'] for detail-dialog assertions instead.
rca of the issue: Original bug: ReturnsTab.jsx had hardcoded 'Credit' wording and emerald colors in detail dialog & create form even when return_type === 'missing'. Fix introduced isMissingReturn / noteWord / amountAccentText flags derived from selectedReturn.return_type, conditional ternaries for header text ('Credit/Unit' vs 'Debit/Unit', 'Total Credit' vs 'Total Debit'), color classes (emerald-* vs amber-*) and footer button label ('Create Credit Note' vs 'Create Debit Note'). Status badge label is transformed via .replace('Credit','Debit') for missing-type returns. Verified end-to-end in the live preview.
